trend: Short-term correction within a longer-term uptrend; S&P 500 off ~3% from recent highs, NAS100 ~4% off peak.
sentiment: Defensive and headline-driven. Oil shock from US-Iran conflict, VIX back near 25, and February CPI in-line at 2.4% YoY provide modest relief — but forward inflation fears from energy persist. Risk appetite fragile.
fed_policy: data dependent — FOMC meets 18-19 Mar; CPI in-line reduces urgency to hike but oil-driven inflation risk keeps cuts off table
next_major_catalyst: FOMC decision (18-19 Mar 2026) + Adobe earnings (12 Mar, after close)

strategy_adjustments.macro-volatility: Prime window — most events produce outsized moves in high-vol. Focus on FOMC (18-19 Mar), US jobless claims (12 Mar), and ongoing US-Iran / oil headlines. Widen stops on directional index trades or reduce size. Favour volatility breakouts on XAU/USD and USOIL; fade first-spike overreactions on US500 and NAS100.
strategy_adjustments.earnings-momentum: Active but downshifted: only high-conviction setups. Oracle gap follow-through is the cleanest current play. Adobe (12 Mar AC) is a pre-earnings momentum candidate — exit before print. Avoid holding leveraged positions through the FOMC window (16-19 Mar).
strategy_adjustments.post-shock: Highest opportunity window given elevated vol and recent sharp single-name moves. Require 1-2 days of price stabilisation before entry. Focus on quality names with intact fundamentals; avoid catching falling knives in structurally challenged sectors.
strategy_adjustments.crypto: BTC only at moderate conviction; high-beta alts (SOL, ADA) reduced to low or removed. Sub-2x leverage; BTC holding near 70k with improving momentum. Altcoin allocation minimal until VIX retreats below 20.
strategy_adjustments.pair-trades: Relatively regime-neutral — best strategy in high-vol. Tighten spread stops on both legs. Favour quality-vs-reset pairs (LLY/NVO) and sector-rotation pairs (Energy long / Tech short). Reduce expected holding days given headline risk.

notes: US February CPI printed in-line at +2.4% YoY / +0.3% MoM this morning (11 Mar), removing a hawkish upside surprise risk. However, the US-Iran conflict and Strait of Hormuz disruption have pushed Brent to $87-92/bbl and WTI to $83-84, creating a forward energy inflation overhang the Fed will address at the 18-19 Mar FOMC. Oracle beat Q3 estimates sharply (EPS $1.79 vs $1.71 est, revenue +22% YoY) and gapped up ~10% after hours on 10 Mar. Adobe reports 12 Mar after close. DAX recovered to ~23,800-23,935 after Middle East de-escalation hopes; DE40 down ~4% over the past month. Session note (Revolut execution overlay): enter macro trades only during London open (09-11 CET) or NY overlap (14:30-17:00 CET); avoid Asian session entries. Seasonal overlay: mid-March is a neutral seasonal window; Q1 earnings season begins re-activating in late March.
